hallucinations.description: The paper cites 'Chang, X. and Wang, R. (2025). Language Accommodation in Human-AI Conversations: Bidirectional Style Adaptation Across Cultures. Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence.' This appears to be a fabricated citation. No such paper by these authors with this exact title can be verified as published or accepted at AAAI 2025. The paper is cited to support claims about word-level bidirectional style adaptation in human-AI conversations across cultural contexts.
hallucinations.evidence: Chang, X. and Wang, R. (2025). Language Accommodation in Human-AI Conversations: Bidirectional Style Adaptation Across Cultures. Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence.

hallucinations.description: The paper cites 'Shen, H., Knearem, T., Thakkar, D., Pataranutaporn, P., Sinha, A. K., Shi, Y., Liang, J. T., Ahmad, L., Mitra, T., Myers, B. A., and Li, Y. (2025). Human-AI Interaction Alignment: Designing, Evaluating, and Evolving Value-Centered AI For Reciprocal Human-AI Futures. arXiv preprint arXiv:2512.21551.' The arXiv ID 2512.21551 would correspond to a paper from December 2025, which is a future date relative to the paper's apparent writing date. While the workshop task description mentions a 'bidirectional human-AI alignment' framework by Shen et al., the specific arXiv ID cannot be verified and may be fabricated.
hallucinations.evidence: Shen, H., Knearem, T., Thakkar, D., Pataranutaporn, P., Sinha, A. K., Shi, Y., Liang, J. T., Ahmad, L., Mitra, T., Myers, B. A., and Li, Y. (2025). Human-AI Interaction Alignment: Designing, Evaluating, and Evolving Value-Centered AI For Reciprocal Human-AI Futures. arXiv preprint arXiv:2512.21551.

hallucinations.description: The paper reports in Table 4 that the directional C_sem values for h-m2 differ substantially from the C_sem values reported for h-m1 in Table 2. For example, Table 2 reports C_sem for T1 (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) as 0.3036, but Table 4 reports C_sem^{H<-A} for the same model and tier as 0.0853. The paper does not adequately explain this discrepancy. Looking at the code, the h-m2 C_sem computation uses a topic-matched KNN shuffle baseline rather than a random shuffle baseline, which would produce different (smaller) values. However, the paper's notation 'C_sem^{H<-A}' in Table 4 uses the same C_sem notation as in the main definition, potentially misleading readers into thinking these are comparable quantities when they use different baselines.
hallucinations.evidence: Table 2 shows T1 all-MiniLM-L6-v2 C_sem = 0.3036, while Table 4 shows C_sem^{H<-A} for the same model/tier = 0.0853

hallucinations.description: The paper reports h-m4 mediation regression results in Table 6 with n=3,000 per model, but the actual experiment results JSON (regression_results.json) shows nobs=310,786 for all three models. The paper claims 'n = 3,000 per model' which contradicts the actual experimental output. This appears to be a fabricated sample size in the paper text.
hallucinations.evidence: Table 6: OLS Mediation Regression (n = 3,000 per model) vs regression_results.json showing 'nobs': 310786 for all three models

hallucinations.description: The paper reports in Table 6 that beta_PM for all-mpnet-base-v2 is '+6.76e-05' (positive), but the actual regression_results.json shows beta_pm for all-mpnet-base-v2 is -5.632372009365013e-05 (negative). The sign is reversed in the paper.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper Table 6: all-mpnet-base-v2 beta_PM = +6.76e-05 vs regression_results.json: 'beta_pm': -5.632372009365013e-05

hallucinations.description: The paper reports R-squared values of 0.007 and 0.012 in Table 6, but the actual regression_results.json shows R-squared values of approximately 0.0026 (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), 0.0031 (paraphrase-MiniLM-L6-v2), and 0.0020 (all-mpnet-base-v2) for the full model. The paper's reported R-squared values are inflated by roughly 2-6x compared to the actual results.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper Table 6: R-squared ranges from 0.007 to 0.012 vs regression_results.json: r_squared values of 0.00265, 0.00307, 0.00198

overall_assessment: The paper contains several hallucinations of varying severity. Two citations appear to be nonexistent or unverifiable (Chang & Wang 2025, Shen et al. 2025 with suspicious arXiv ID). More critically, the h-m4 mediation regression results in Table 6 contain multiple discrepancies with the actual experimental output: the sample size is reported as 3,000 when it was actually 310,786; one beta coefficient has a reversed sign; and R-squared values are inflated. There is also a methodological inconsistency where the h-m2 directional C_sem values use a different baseline than the main C_sem definition without clear explanation. The core findings (h-e1, h-m1, h-m2, h-m3) appear to be supported by the experimental logs and code, but the h-m4 results as reported in the paper text do not match the actual experimental outputs.
